title: Eschweileria tahitensis
intro: Eschweileria tahitensis, also known as Tahitian wood fern or Tahitian shield fern, is a species of fern native to French Polynesia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Eschweileria tahitensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Polynesian medicine, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
